gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: releaseFractalFunds() public with no access control allows pre-call to inflate interval allocation and extract excess TitanX incentive
candidate.claimed_root_cause: releaseFractalFunds() is public with only a time guard; calling it standalone before swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn inflates totalTitanXBurn before the burnTitanXIntervalUpdate modifier snapshots amountAllocated, yielding a larger incentive transfer to the attacker
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker extracts approximately 154,258 extra TitanX per eligible fractal release window beyond their entitled incentive

live_preconditions.name: block.timestamp >= lastFractalRelease + FRACTAL_RELEASE_INTERVAL
live_preconditions.observed_value: lastFractalRelease=1780682423 (~2026-06-05); FRACTAL_RELEASE_INTERVAL=9504000 (110 days); eligible after timestamp 1790186423 (~2026-09-23); NOT currently met as of 2026-07-14 capture (~71 days remaining); all structural values confirmed present

live_preconditions.name: currInterval.amountBurned == 0 for lastTitanXBurnIntervalNumber
live_preconditions.observed_value: lastTitanXBurnIntervalNumber=53087; direct slot value not in live_context; no BuyAndBurn events in 10000-block sample window suggests interval is unexecuted

live_preconditions.name: DAILY_ALLOCATION_TITAN_X_BURNING (live value used in calculation)
live_preconditions.observed_value: 200 (2% in basis points; owner has updated from contract default of 100)

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Declared public with no access control beyond time guard; any caller can invoke it standalone to move fractalTitanX into totalTitanXBurn before the interval modifier reads totalTitanXBurn

Modifier calls _intervalUpdateTitanXForBurning() before the function body via the _; pattern; totalTitanXBurn is therefore read at modifier time, before the internal releaseFractalFunds() call inside swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn at L249-251

dailyAllocation = totalTitanXBurn * DAILY_ALLOCATION_TITAN_X_BURNING / BPS_DENOM; reads totalTitanXBurn at modifier execution time; if releaseFractalFunds() was pre-called, totalTitanXBurn is inflated by fractalTitanX (~74B TitanX), producing a larger amountAllocated

amountAllocated is read from currInterval set by modifier (L233); incentive = amountAllocated * INCENTIVE_FEE / BPS_DENOM (L236-237); internal releaseFractalFunds() at L249-251 fires after allocation is snapshotted and will revert if pre-called (lastFractalRelease just updated), preventing double-release but not preventing the inflated incentive
INCENTIVE_FEE=150 (1.5%), FRACTAL_RELEASE_INTERVAL=110 days, BPS_DENOM=10000, INTERVALS_PER_DAY=144, INTERVAL_TIME=10 minutes; govern incentive and allocation arithmetic

attacker_inputs: standalone call to releaseFractalFunds() with no arguments
call to swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn(deadline) in same block or immediately after
call_sequence: 2. attacker.call(TorusBuyAndProcess.releaseFractalFunds()): moves fractalTitanX (74043750293778189300123718320) into totalTitanXBurn; sets lastFractalRelease = block.timestamp; fractalTitanX = 0

3. attacker.call(TorusBuyAndProcess.swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn(deadline)): modifier burnTitanXIntervalUpdate fires _intervalUpdateTitanXForBurning() which calls _calculateIntervalsTitanXBurn() reading inflated totalTitanXBurn; amountAllocated is larger; incentive = amountAllocated * 150 / 10000 is larger; internal releaseFractalFunds() at L249 reverts (lastFractalRelease just updated, time guard blocks it); attacker receives inflated incentive via safeTransfer(TITAN_X_ADDRESS, msg.sender, incentive) at L254
state_after: After releaseFractalFunds(): totalTitanXBurn = 464091268443215444654955710007; fractalTitanX = 0; lastFractalRelease = block.timestamp
Without pre-call: dailyAllocation = 390047518149437255354831991687 * 200 / 10000; amountPerInterval ~54173266; incentiveBaseline ~812598 TitanX

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: TitanX tokens transferred to attacker as incentive via TransferHelper.safeTransfer(TITAN_X_ADDRESS, msg.sender, incentive) at L254 of swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: ~154,258 extra TitanX per exploitation window (~110 days) beyond what they would receive by calling swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn without the pre-call
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol totalTitanXBurn pool is reduced by the inflated amountAllocated; the extra ~154,258 TitanX comes from the protocol burn pool and is transferred to the attacker rather than being used for Torus buy-and-burn
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: The protocol intends fractal release to occur as a side-effect inside swap/distribute calls AFTER the interval allocation is snapshotted (L249-251 fires after L233 reads amountAllocated). By pre-calling releaseFractalFunds() standalone, the attacker causes the modifier to observe a post-release inflated totalTitanXBurn, yielding a larger amountAllocated and thus a larger incentive than the protocol design intends for that interval. The nonReentrant guard on swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn does not prevent this two-call sequence.

setup: vm.createSelectFork('mainnet', RECENT_BLOCK) where RECENT_BLOCK is a block with timestamp near 2026-07-14
vm.warp(1780682423 + 110 days + 1) to satisfy time guard
transaction_sequence: BASELINE RUN: fork reset + warp; prank attacker; call contract.swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn(deadline); record incentiveBaseline = IERC20(TITAN_X_ADDRESS).balanceOf(attacker) - attackerTitanXBefore

ATTACK RUN: fork reset + warp; prank attacker; call contract.releaseFractalFunds(); call contract.swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn(deadline); record incentiveAttack = IERC20(TITAN_X_ADDRESS).balanceOf(attacker) - attackerTitanXBefore
expected_assertions: assertGt(incentiveAttack, incentiveBaseline, 'Pre-call to releaseFractalFunds inflates incentive')
assertApproxEqRel(incentiveAttack - incentiveBaseline, 154258e18, 0.05e18, 'Delta within 5% of calculated value')
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If incentiveAttack == incentiveBaseline, the interval update reads totalTitanXBurn after the internal releaseFractalFunds() call (ordering assumption is wrong) and this finding must be REJECTED. If releaseFractalFunds() reverts when called standalone at the warped timestamp, the time precondition calculation is wrong and the finding must be re-evaluated.
